agntux-core.tagline: The AgntUX orchestrator. Triages action items and queries your knowledge store.
agntux-core.description:
Installs first; everything else hangs off it. Maintains your `<agntux project root>/`
knowledge store, triages action items per your `user.md` preferences,
and keeps `_index.md` files in sync as ingest plugins write new data.

supported_prompts.purpose: First-run interview and schema bootstrap. Creates <agntux project root>/user.md and the tenant schema.
Edit your AgntUX profile — preferences, glossary, identity, sources, action-worthy/noise rules.
Capture per-plugin instructions. With a {plugin-slug} runs the install-time / on-demand teach interview.
Daily action-item digest. Recommended scheduled task at Daily 08:00.
Review or edit the tenant schema. Bare form re-walks; `review {slug}` re-reviews a contract; `edit` drives a user-initiated change.
Cross-plugin sync alias. `/agntux-sync {plugin-slug}` re-dispatches to the per-plugin sync command.
Catch-all for natural-language queries — entity lookups, time-window questions, topic queries, meeting prep, status edits.
Daily pattern detection over recently-resolved action items. Recommended scheduled task at Daily 16:00.

ui_components.name: triage
ui_components.title: Action item triage
ui_components.purpose: The primary inline UI. Shows priority-sorted open action items with snooze / dismiss / done buttons.

ui_components.name: entity-browser
ui_components.title: Entity browser
ui_components.purpose: Browse and pivot between people, companies, projects, and topics in your knowledge store.

agntux-slack.tagline: Turn your Slack workspace into an AgntUX knowledge store, with safe drafts.
agntux-slack.description:
Watches every Slack channel and DM you have access to, captures DMs,
@mentions, and active threads, and extracts people, companies, projects,
and topics as AgntUX entities. Action items surface response-needed
signals, deadlines, and decisions buried in long threads. On request,
drafts replies (or thread summaries) in chat and asks you to confirm
before sending — the actual `slack_send_message`, `slack_schedule_message`,
and `slack_create_canvas` calls only happen after you say "yes".
data_ingested: DMs (1:1) and group DMs in your workspace
@mentions of you in any channel you have access to
Threads where you are OP or have replied
Channel messages in any channel you have authored in recently
Pinned messages and canvases referenced from monitored channels

supported_prompts.purpose: Run an agntux-slack pass now or on schedule. Recommended: every 30 min on weekdays 7am–10pm; chat is time-sensitive during work hours but doesn't need overnight or weekend runs.

entity_subtypes.description: Slack users — DM partners, channel co-authors, mentioned colleagues. Email from profile is the cross-source alias.
Organizations referenced in Slack — vendors, customers, partners surfaced via shared links or named in messages.
Workstreams or codenames mentioned in channels and threads (often resolved via user.md → # Glossary).
Recurring themes, products, or initiatives surfaced across multiple Slack threads.

action_classes.description: A Slack message carrying an explicit due date or time-bound ask.
A DM, @mention, vote/poll, or thread reply that expects a response from the user.
An informational signal extracted from a Slack thread (decision, status update, pinned message).
Outage/incident/security signal in a monitored ops or security channel; @here / @channel pings to high-stakes channels.
Competitor or market signal in a sales/marketing channel; prospect activity or partnership cues.
Escape hatch for items that don't fit the above; requires reason_detail.

proposed_schema.cursor_semantics: Single JSON map (one line) under `cursor`. Two key shapes: `<channel_id>` for channel cursors (newest parent ts), `<channel_id>#<thread_ts>` for thread cursors (newest reply ts).
proposed_schema.source_id_format: `<channel_id>#<thread_ts>` for thread-rooted artefacts; `<channel_id>#<ts>` for non-threaded messages.

plugin-toolkit.tagline: Authoring + maintainer toolkit for AgntUX plugins — skills, agents, runbooks.
plugin-toolkit.description:
Authoring and maintainer toolkit. `/plugin-toolkit:author` is a slim
orchestrator delegating to 7 specialist agents (manifest, ingest
prompt, source semantics, draft flow, tests, invariants, release).
`/plugin-toolkit:maintain` inlines maintainer runbooks (PR review,
rollback, kill-switch, canonical-hook updates, secret rotation).
Auto-triggers on plugin metadata edits; load-bearing rules stay
inline so every agent inherits them.

supported_prompts.purpose: Authoring orchestrator. Auto-triggers on plugins/{slug}/ edits; delegates to 7 specialist agents.
Maintainer runbooks: PR review, rollback, kill-switch, canonical-hook update, secret rotation, CI map.
